name: Alara Georgetown

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against Alara Georgetown's current restaurant-issued menu index, rendered brunch route, and six currently linked PDFs reviewed on 2026-07-14.
The current catalog contains 100 food and nonalcoholic formulations represented by 156 meal-period presentations across 25 source sections.

Ninety-four frozen rows represent current formulations; six current formulations are missing from the frozen output: Taste of Alara for the Entire Party, Fries, Coffee, Matmazel, Alara Blush, and Stella Artois 0.0 Non-Alcoholic.
Seven frozen rows are course headings, component text, or fused choice artifacts; three isolated alcohol rows are scope artifacts from an otherwise omitted alcohol list.
Current meal-period names and presentations remain distinct where the source does, including Tzatziki versus Tzatziki Dip and Beef Pide versus Ground Beef Pide.
Alara's restaurant-issued menus publish selected fixed ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix, complete recipes, or cross-contact policy.

Seventy-one current formulations have positive signals supported by fixed published ingredients, mandatory named formats, or Alara's own published component recipes; 29 remain unavailable rather than inferred.
Twenty-eight frozen rows underreported current positive ingredient signals; 42 frozen rows are verified and 24 are accurately unavailable.
Vegetarian, vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free labels are not converted into negative claims, and the page-wide raw-food warning is not assigned to individual products.
Configurable later courses, cold-mezze choices, juice choices, and optional additions are not promoted to fixed allergens.
The frozen generic website extraction flattened dinner, lunch, brunch, dessert, catering, and cocktail surfaces into inconsistent categories and did not retain presentation structure.

It promoted four course headings and parenthetical component text, fused Homemade Ice Cream and Kunefe into one nonexistent item, duplicated Lentil Soup as a package choice, and retained three isolated alcohol rows while omitting the rest of that list.
It omitted six current formulations and 28 positive allergen signals from fixed text, including bulgur, flatbread, pita, dough, pastry, bechamel, nuts, sesame, egg, and dairy terms.
The correction stores 100 canonical formulations with 156 source presentations, source-bounded positive evidence, no may-contain claims, and 29 explicitly unavailable rows.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the current restaurant-issued menu index and verified that it currently links the reviewed dinner, lunch, brunch, dessert, catering, and cocktail PDFs.

Captured, hashed, rendered, and visually reviewed every page of all six current restaurant-issued PDFs; inventoried every food and nonalcoholic row, description, section, package choice, and alcohol boundary.
notes: Each PDF is retained under data/restaurant-verification/artifacts/alara-georgetown-dc and independently SHA-256 anchored in the evidence manifest.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the restaurant's rendered brunch route and reconciled its structured and visible item names and descriptions with the current brunch PDF.
Reviewed all current menu surfaces for allergen disclosure semantics; found fixed ingredient text and dietary labels but no complete matrix, recipes, or item-level cross-contact disclosure.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog flattened six current menu surfaces, omitted six current formulations, lost meal-period presentation structure, and retained ten heading, fused-choice, or scope artifacts.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 100 current formulations with 156 source presentations, restored all six omissions, and removed only the seven structural/fusion artifacts and three isolated alcohol-scope artifacts documented by the source layouts.

findings.summary: Twenty-eight frozen rows omitted positive allergens supported by current fixed ingredient text, mandatory named formats, or Alara's own published component recipes.
findings.resolution: Recomputed 71 supported positive formulations, verified 42 frozen rows, retained 29 current formulations as unavailable, and assigned no unsupported may-contain or negative claims.

findings.summary: The generic parser lost section, course, presentation, and alcohol-scope boundaries and only partially interpreted the restaurant's published ingredient descriptions.
findings.resolution: Prepared a guarded current-menu manifest correction with exact item/presentation/category counts, source aliases, configurable-choice semantics, and focused regression invariants.

repairs.summary: Prepared and focused-tested a 100-formulation, 156-presentation Alara catalog across all current restaurant-issued food and nonalcoholic surfaces, with 71 supported positive rows, 29 unavailable rows, and every frozen menu/allergen defect reconciled.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=tmp/restaurant-verification/alara-isolated.generated.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 100 items, 17 canonical categories, 71 positive rows, 29 unavailable rows, all six current additions, and removal of all documented artifacts)

notes: Current corrected catalog counts: 100 formulations, 156 presentations, 25 source sections, 71 positive rows, and 29 unavailable rows.
